title: Malcolm-Jamal Warner's widow sues his mother over his estate

title: Judge approves a $1.5B Anthropic settlement over books used to train Claude

title: Maduro trial date set for June 2027 as drug trafficking case against ousted Venezuelan leader proceeds

Suspect covered in blood punches Hernando County deputy in face as unconscious woman lies nearby: Sheriff
summary: A deputy on proactive patrol was forced into a violent confrontation Tuesday while responding to a severe domestic dispute in the Riverdale neighborhood, according to the Hernando County Sheriff’s Office.

title: Pasco County human-trafficking and prostitution ring leads to 8 arrests: Police
summary: Eight people face criminal charges following a multi-day human trafficking and prostitution sting in Pasco County, authorities announced.

title: Tampa film lab sees Gen Z fueling film photography comeback
summary: In a world where most pictures live on phones, Coastal Film Lab is helping keep traditional photography alive.

title: Florida memory care facility files for bankruptcy after $1.5M court judgment
summary: A Pasco County memory care facility filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy weeks after a judge ordered it to pay a $1.5 million award to a resident's family in a wrongful death lawsuit.

title: Adolf Hitler's birthplace in Austria reopens as a police station to deter Nazi pilgrimages
summary: Adolf Hitler's birthplace in Austria was officially inaugurated as a police station on Wednesday in an effort to destroy the building's appeal as a site of pilgrimage for people who glorify the Nazi dictator.

title: NYC Legionnaires’ outbreak claims fifth life as source remains unknown
summary: A fifth person has died in New York City’s Legionnaires’ outbreak as officials clean dozens of cooling towers and investigate the source.

title: Historic West Virginia flooding washes out roads, knocks out 911 service as rescues continue
summary: West Virginia's governor has declared a state of emergency for all 55 counties following historic flooding Tuesday from a storm that spawned tornadoes and damaging winds from Kentucky to New Jersey.

title: Bucs to wear '76 jerseys for 2026 Creamsicle game
summary: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers announced the team will once again wear the iconic white jerseys from 1976 during the annual "Creamsicle Game" on Dec. 6.
